reason: Pre-modification snapshot: Test: minimal change to isolate 400 cause

parameters.name: list_domains
parameters.description: Lists all available API domains with their category and status. Call this FIRST to discover what APIs are available. Returns domain names, categories (ai, crm, analytics, etc), and auth types.

parameters.name: list_actions
parameters.description: Lists all available actions for a specific API domain. Input: domain (string) - the API domain name from list_domains. Returns action names, HTTP methods, descriptions, and body modes.

parameters.name: get_action_schema
parameters.description: Returns the parameter schema, description, and example request for a specific action. Input: domain (string), action (string). Use this BEFORE execute_action to understand what parameters are needed.

parameters.name: execute_action
parameters.description:
Execute any API action through the gateway. Input: domain (string), action (string), client (string - 'kahuna' or 'ascend'), params (object - action-specific parameters).

PARAM HINTS: For 'passthrough' domains (AI, Apollo, Gong, Semrush, Vercel, etc), params go directly as the API body. For 'handler' domains (HubSpot, Google, Slack, etc), check get_action_schema if unsure about required params.

parameters.name: get_health
parameters.description: Returns the current health status of all gateway APIs. Shows which APIs are healthy, degraded, or down. No input required.

parameters.name: get_cost_summary
parameters.description: Returns AI API token usage and cost data. Optional input: days (number, default 30), provider (string - filter by AI domain), client_id (string - filter by client, non-admin users can only see their own costs). Returns aggregate cost breakdowns by domain and by client.

parameters.name: list_capabilities
parameters.description: Returns available API endpoints for a specific domain from the capability catalog. Input: domain (string, required) - the API domain name; executable_only (boolean, optional, default true) - filter to only executable endpoints. Use this to discover what endpoints are available for Tier 3 passthrough or to understand a domain's capabilities before using execute_passthrough.

parameters.name: execute_passthrough
parameters.description:
Execute a validated passthrough request to any Tier 3 API (AI engines, business APIs with API key auth). The gateway validates the endpoint against the capability catalog, injects credentials, forwards the request, and logs to audit. Use list_capabilities to discover available endpoints first.

Parameters (JSON):
- domain (required): API domain name (e.g., 'deepseek', 'anthropic', 'notion', 'github')
- path (required): Endpoint path (e.g., '/v1/chat/completions')
- method (required): HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH)
- body (optional): Request body as JSON object
- query_params (optional): Query string parameters as JSON object
- headers (optional): Additional headers (auth headers are injected automatically)
